chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents a master product in the catalog, uniquely identified by PRODUCT_ID and described by its product attributes and name.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO |
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |
| PARENT_CATEGORY_ID | INT | Parent category for hierarchy | YES | | | PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO |
| SKU | VARCHAR(50) | Stock Keeping Unit code | NO |
| PRODUCT_NAME | VARCHAR(300) | Product display name | NO |
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO | | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment) | | CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO |
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO | | Product Relationships:
- A Product belongs to exactly one Category | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master(Cust Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A customer master data entity/table whose records are uniquely identified by the CUST_ID key and referenced by related tables such as SALES_ORDER_HDR.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr
entities_post_resolution.definition: A foreign-key column CUST_ID in the SALES_ORDER_HDR table that references CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
FOREIGN KEYS**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)
 | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Addresses
entities_post_resolution.definition: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Customer Relationships:
- A Customer has one or more Customer Addresses (not in simplified schema) | # Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses
- Geographic region for tax calculation
- Active/inactive status | Customer Relationships:
- A Customer places zero or more Sales Orders | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A single line item within a sales order that specifies a product, quantity, and unit price at the time of purchase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | **Key Attributes**:
- Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Business Rules**:
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation | **Examples**:
- Line 1: 2 units of Wireless Headphones at €79.99 each | **Examples**:
- Line 2: 1 unit of USB Cable at €12.50 | SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment is a monetary transaction that settles a Sales Order, identified by PAYMENT_ID, linked to exactly one Sales Order, and including the payment amount; a Sales Order may have one or more Payments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Definition**: The monetary transaction that settles the total amount due for a Sales Order. | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Payment amount | **Key Attributes**:
- Confirmation timestamp | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, FAILED, REFUNDED | **Business Rules**:
- Payment must be confirmed before order ships | **Business Rules**:
- Refunds create new payment records with negative amounts | **Business Rules**:
- Multiple payment attempts can exist for one order | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: The shipment represents the physical delivery of products from a warehouse to a customer's address, associated with a single sales order and identified by a unique Shipment ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Definition**: The physical delivery of Products from a Warehouse to a Customer's delivery address. | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Tracking number | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | **Business Rules**:
- Can only be created for confirmed orders with payment | **Business Rules**:
- One order may have multiple partial shipments | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A formal transaction document representing a customer’s order to purchase products, containing a unique order ID, customer reference, date, line items, total value, and a status such as PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, or CANCELLED.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SALES ORDER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sale

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category
entities_post_resolution.definition: Master catalogue of all products available for sale, identified by PRODUCT_ID with product-level relationships.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment) | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | FOREIGN KEYS**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)
 | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

**Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | Product          → TB_PRODUCT

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price At
entities_post_resolution.definition: The price per unit of a product as recorded at the time an order is placed, representing the historical unit price used for order calculations rather than the current price.
synonyms: price at the moment of ordering
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | | CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO |
| UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price in EUR | NO | | **Business Rules**:
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates | **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Customer Identifier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier assigned to each line item within a transaction or document to distinguish individual line items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| CUST_ID | INT | Unique customer identifier | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier

entities_post_resolution.name: Confirmed Orders With Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A state in the order lifecycle representing a customer order that has been confirmed and payment has been completed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Can only be created for confirmed orders with payment | **Business Rules**:
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Sales Order is a business document that records a customer's request to purchase products, tying to exactly one customer and containing one or more line items, each referencing exactly one product with quantity and unit pricing.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SALES ORDER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Sales Order Relationships:

entities_post_resolution.name: Return Policy
entities_post_resolution.definition: Return Policy is a business policy that allows customers to return purchased products within a defined period, typically in exchange for a refund.

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core information about customers, including their identity, contact details, regional assignment, when the record was created, and whether the customer is currently active.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products including identifiers, stock-keeping unit, product name, category association, pricing, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores the hierarchical categories for products, including each category's name and a reference to its parent category.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores metadata about customer sales orders, including identifiers, timing, amounts, status, and payment/delivery timestamps for each order.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores the details of each product line within an order, including the product, quantity, unit price, and line total.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of customer payments linked to orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments associated with customer orders, including when the shipment occurred, where it is fulfilled from, tracking details, and current status.
